proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months']); OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = +0.785 (p < 1e-300). Linear OLS slope = +0.1765 PFS-months per unit feature_015 (t=266.5, p<1e-300). Single-variable R^2 = 0.587. Mean PFS rises monotonically across feature_015 deciles from 1.33 mo (feature_015<=52.3) to 7.45 mo (feature_015>77.8).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is statistically independent of the other covariates (no meaningful correlation with the binary or continuous lab features), consistent with it acting as an independent prognostic axis rather than a proxy for another measured variable.

analyses.code: Spearman correlations between feature_015 and all 16 continuous/binary covariates; ANOVA across binary feature levels.
analyses.result_summary: All Spearman |r| < 0.011 with continuous labs (max |r|=0.0102 vs feature_013, p=0.023). All ANOVA p-values comparing feature_015 mean across binary feature levels are non-significant (smallest p=0.066 vs feature_020). feature_015 thus carries prognostic information not captured by any of the other 32 measured variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature_001 (values 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a graded fashion.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_001)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusting for feature_015: feature_001=1 vs 0 = -1.199 mo (p<1e-300); feature_001=2 vs 0 = -2.345 mo (p<1e-300). Unadjusted means: 5.27 / 4.05 / 2.91 mo for levels 0/1/2. Strong dose-response — feature_001 looks like a performance-status-like ordinal predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_014=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_014=0.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind; OLS adjusted for feature_015 + C(feature_001) + other binaries.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_014 effect = -1.352 mo (3.703 vs 5.055; p<1e-300). After multivariable adjustment for feature_015, feature_001, and other binaries the effect is essentially unchanged at -1.354 mo (t=-159.3, p<1e-300). feature_014 is one of the strongest binary prognostic factors in the cohort and is balanced across feature_015 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_027=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_027=0 (overall main effect).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind; multivariable OLS.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_027 effect = +0.972 mo (5.089 vs 4.117; p=1.7e-216). Adjusted for feature_015, feature_001, and other binaries, effect = +0.935 mo (t=88.6, p<1e-300). Prevalence 20.0%. Largest favorable binary main effect in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_023=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_006=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006=0.
Patients with feature_022=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_022=0.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS adjusted for feature_015, feature_001 + other binaries.
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 adjusted effect = -0.355 mo (t=-40.2, p<1e-300). Prevalence 41.8%.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 adjusted effect = -0.281 mo (t=-31.8, p<1e-300). Prevalence 35.3%.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 adjusted effect = -0.361 mo (t=-17.5, p<1e-300). Prevalence 4.5% (rare).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, several continuous lab-like features carry independent signal: feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, albumin-like) is favorable, feature_025 (range 0-24.6, NLR-like) is adverse, and feature_005 (range 0-777, CEA-like) is adverse.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014 + feature_027 + feature_023 + feature_006 + feature_022 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_005 + ... lab features', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: feature_019 +0.469 mo per unit (t=61.0, p<1e-300); feature_025 -0.076 mo per unit (t=-76.1, p<1e-300); feature_005 -0.005 mo per unit (t=-21.0, p<1e-25); feature_021 -0.0004 per unit (small adverse, p<1e-18); feature_032 borderline (-0.0008, p=0.057). Other labs (feature_017, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009) all non-significant after adjustment. Full multivariable R^2 = 0.859.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 is associated with longer pfs_months on average; the rare binary feature_002 has no detectable effect.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS, adjusted.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 unadjusted effect = +0.221 mo (p=7.3e-04, prevalence 3.0%); after multivariable adjustment effect shrinks to +0.010 (p=0.70) — its apparent benefit is largely captured by other variables. feature_002 (prevalence 0.5%) has effectively no effect (+0.04, p=0.79). feature_028, feature_016, feature_024, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_031 (sex-like) are all non-significant after adjustment (all |coef|<0.04, all p>0.10).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031 (binary, ~45% prevalence) — a sex-like indicator — is independent of pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on pfs by feature_031; multivariable OLS coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_031 effect = -0.006 mo (p=0.77). Adjusted coefficient +0.007 (p=0.44). feature_031 is not associated with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The adverse effect of feature_014 is broadly homogeneous across major patient strata (no important treatment-effect modifier exists for feature_014).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests by feature_001 (ECOG-like), feature_015 tertile, feature_005 quartile, feature_019 quartile, feature_025 quartile, plus interaction screen in OLS.
analyses.result_summary: feature_014 effect ranges narrowly: -1.40 to -1.27 across feature_001 levels; -1.28 to -1.40 across feature_015 tertiles; -1.30 to -1.38 across feature_005 quartiles; -1.34 to -1.37 across feature_019 quartiles; -1.28 to -1.38 across feature_025 quartiles. The largest standardized interaction term in OLS is feature_014:feature_015 (-0.0067 per unit, p=1.1e-17), implying a slightly more adverse effect at older feature_015. No meaningful subgroup reverses or eliminates the feature_014 penalty.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_027 is concentrated in the feature_006=0 stratum and is null when feature_006=1.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests; OLS interaction.
analyses.result_summary: In feature_006=0 (n=32,343): feature_027 effect = +1.480 mo (p=3.7e-274). In feature_006=1 (n=17,657): feature_027 effect = +0.026 mo (p=0.52). Interaction coefficient feature_027:feature_006 = -1.461 (p<1e-300). Strong qualitative effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_027 is concentrated in the feature_023=0 stratum and is null when feature_023=1.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests; OLS interaction.
analyses.result_summary: In feature_023=0 (n=29,102): feature_027 effect = +1.656 mo (p=6.1e-293). In feature_023=1 (n=20,898): feature_027 effect = +0.027 mo (p=0.46). Interaction coefficient feature_027:feature_023 = -1.635 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022=1 (rare, ~5%) suppresses the favorable effect of feature_027.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests; OLS interaction.
analyses.result_summary: In feature_022=0 (n=47,728): feature_027 effect = +1.027 mo (p=5e-226). In feature_022=1 (n=2,272): feature_027 effect = -0.156 mo (p=0.13). Interaction feature_027:feature_022 = -0.988 (p=1.5e-102).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are simultaneously feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 (a 'triple-biomarker-negative' subgroup; n=17,372, 34.7% of cohort) experience a much larger feature_027 PFS benefit than patients with any of these features positive.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests on the joint subgroup vs everyone else; combination table over the 8 cells of (f006,f023,f022).
analyses.result_summary: Triple-negative subgroup (n=17,372): feature_027 effect = +2.758 mo (p<1e-300). Any-positive complement (n=32,628): feature_027 effect = +0.030 mo (p=0.29). Across the 8-cell combination table, only the 000 cell shows a meaningful feature_027 benefit; all other cells have effect within +/-0.20 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-biomarker-negative subgroup, the feature_027 benefit is further concentrated in patients with low feature_005 (CEA-like marker), with the benefit attenuating monotonically as feature_005 rises.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis: feature_027 effect within triple-negative by feature_005 quartile and by sliding cutpoints.
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-negative subgroup, feature_027 effect by feature_005 quartile: Q1 (0.04-1.96): +5.030 mo (n=4,342); Q2 (1.97-4.44): +5.037 mo (n=4,314); Q3 (4.45-10.07): +0.674 mo (n=4,446); Q4 (10.08-442.29): -0.013 mo (n=4,270). Sharp drop at feature_005 ~ 4.5; below this threshold the benefit is essentially constant ~ +5 mo, above it the benefit collapses. Sliding-cutoff analysis shows the +5 mo plateau holds for any cutpoint between 1.0 and 5.0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 modifies the feature_027 effect in the full cohort but does not add to the subgroup definition once the triple-biomarker-negative + low feature_005 criteria are imposed.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis: feature_027 effect by feature_011 (full cohort and within refined subgroup).
analyses.result_summary: Full cohort: feature_027 effect is +0.941 mo when feature_011=0 vs +1.929 mo when feature_011=1 (interaction OLS p=9e-57). However within the refined (triple-neg & feature_005<4.44) subgroup: feature_027 effect = +5.017 (feature_011=0, n=8,187) vs +5.245 (feature_011=1, n=453); difference 0.23 mo, not significant. feature_011's apparent modifier effect is largely a marginal artifact of feature_011 being enriched among triple-negative low-feature_005 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 as a continuous interaction term modifies the feature_027 effect (each additional unit of feature_005 reduces the feature_027 benefit).

analyses.code: OLS with feature_027:feature_005 term, full multivariable backbone.
analyses.result_summary: Continuous interaction coefficient feature_027:feature_005 = -0.0258 PFS-mo per unit feature_005 (p<1e-300). Combined with the threshold finding above, feature_005 acts as a near-binary modifier with a sharp transition around 4-5 units.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<4.44, the feature_027 benefit is homogeneous across feature_015 (age-like) tertiles.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests by feature_015 tertile within the refined subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup (n=8,640), feature_027 effect by feature_015 tertile: T1 (30.0-60.6, n=2,905): +4.925 mo; T2 (60.7-69.3, n=2,861): +5.073 mo; T3 (69.4-90.0, n=2,874): +5.123 mo. Effect virtually identical across age tertiles — feature_015 does not modulate the feature_027 benefit in the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the feature_027 benefit is also homogeneous across feature_001 (ECOG-like ordinal) levels.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests by feature_001 within the refined subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup (n=8,640), feature_027 effect by feature_001: level 0 (n=3,089): +4.909 mo; level 1 (n=4,247): +5.121 mo; level 2 (n=1,304): +4.741 mo. All p<1e-100. Differences across feature_001 are <0.4 mo and not consistent with a meaningful modifier in the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The adverse main effect of feature_014 persists at full magnitude inside the feature_027 favorable subgroup — i.e., feature_014 does not become benign in the same patients where feature_027 is most effective.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test inside the refined subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_027 favorable subgroup (n=8,640): feature_014 effect = -1.405 mo (p=1e-110). Effect is essentially identical to the cohort-wide feature_014 estimate (-1.36 mo). feature_014 acts as an additive prognostic penalty independent of feature_027 effectiveness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A formal interaction model with the indicator I(triple-neg & feature_005<4.44) reproduces the +5 mo treatment-effect concentration: feature_027 contributes ~+0.07 mo outside the subgroup and the subgroup-by-treatment interaction contributes another ~+4.91 mo, summing to ~+5 mo total benefit in-subgroup.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_027*_subg + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Estimated coefficients: feature_027 = +0.0701 (p=2e-74); _subg = -0.0054 (p=0.19); feature_027:_subg = +4.910 (p<1e-300). Outside subgroup, feature_027 effect ~ +0.07 mo. Inside subgroup, total feature_027 effect ~ +4.98 mo. Model R^2 = 0.93.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the favorable subgroup, the residual feature_027 effect is small but statistically detectable in the very large complement (~ +0.10 mo).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind in complement of the subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=41,360): feature_027=1 mean=4.218 (n=8,240), feature_027=0 mean=4.123 (n=33,120), diff=+0.095 mo, p=3.05e-04. Statistically significant due to large n but clinically negligible (~3 days).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The adverse effect of feature_014 is universal across every binary/ordinal stratum tested: every subgroup with adequate size shows feature_014 effect between -1.24 and -1.85 months.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of feature_014 across every level of every binary and ordinal feature.
analyses.result_summary: Across all 39 strata with n>100 (every level of every binary/ordinal feature), feature_014 effect is uniformly negative: most extreme = -1.852 (feature_002=1, n=251) and least extreme = -1.240 (feature_011=1, n=1,506). No subgroup reverses or eliminates the feature_014 penalty. feature_014 is best modeled as a uniformly adverse main-effect predictor with no clinically meaningful effect modifier in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 and feature_006 act both as adverse main effects AND as suppressors of the feature_027 benefit, suggesting they belong to a single biological resistance axis along with feature_022.

analyses.code: Stratified comparison of effect modification across f006, f023, f022; main-effect coefficients in adjusted OLS.
analyses.result_summary: All three features (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022) have qualitatively identical patterns: a modest direct adverse main effect (-0.28 to -0.36 mo) plus very strong suppression of feature_027 benefit (interaction coefs -1.46, -1.64, -0.99 respectively). When ANY of the three is positive, the feature_027 effect collapses to ~0. They behave like markers of a shared resistance phenotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis: feature_027 confers a large favorable PFS benefit (~+5 months) only in patients meeting ALL of the following four criteria — feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<4.44; outside this subgroup feature_027 has essentially no effect on pfs_months. The unfavorable values that suppress the treatment effect are feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, and feature_005 >= ~4.44.

analyses.code: Final stratified comparison of feature_027 inside vs outside the proposed subgroup; saturated OLS with feature_027 * subgroup interaction term.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_022=0 & feature_005<4.44; n=8,640, 17.3% of cohort): feature_027=1 mean PFS = 9.115 mo (n=1,782), feature_027=0 mean PFS = 4.084 mo (n=6,858), difference = +5.030 mo (p<1e-300). Complement (n=41,360): feature_027 effect = +0.095 mo (p=3e-04). Interaction coefficient in adjusted OLS: feature_027:subgroup = +4.91 (p<1e-300). The four predicates that define the subgroup are jointly necessary — relaxing any single criterion (e.g., letting feature_005 go to 6) materially attenuates the benefit (drops to +4.55 by feature_005<6, +3.62 by feature_005<10).
